title: T-101 — enable drift-detect strict mode (extra_in_db) after dept_code → table registry lands (FT-041)
prompt:
# T-101 — enable drift-detect strict mode (extra_in_db) after dept_code → table registry lands (FT-041)

PRD: `docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §6
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`
Original carry-forward ID: **FT-041** (from `_meta/audits/2026-05-07-foundation-final-carry-forward-backlog.md`)
Severity: **P2** (S effort)

## Goal
drift-detect strict mode enabled; extra_in_db rows cause non-zero exit.

## Implementation contract
1. drift-detect.ts: extra_in_db = registry.read(); fail if any unregistered tables remain.
2. Test seeds a stub registry + DB with one extra table; assert exit non-zero.
3. Test with clean DB: exit 0.

## Files (scope)
- scripts/drift-detect.ts [modify]
- scripts/__tests__/drift-detect-strict.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given an extra DB table not in the registry, when drift-detect runs, then exit code is non-zero and stderr names the extra table.
2. Given a fully-registered DB, when drift-detect runs, then exit code is 0.

## Test strategy
- RED: write drift-detect-strict.test.ts asserting both branches before flag enable.
- Run `pnpm test drift-detect-strict`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not enable strict mode without a complete registry.
- Do not auto-fix (drop the extra table) — only report.

## Closeout evidence
Provide: changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status (per AC), deviations_from_prd, git_status. Use the test-driven-development skill (RED-first) and requesting-code-review at GREEN.

pipeline_inputs.description: Enable extra_in_db strict mode in drift-detect once dept_code table registry lands, so unexpected DB tables fail CI.
pipeline_inputs.details: Today drift-detect only flags missing tables. With the dept_code → table registry available, enable extra_in_db to also flag tables that exist in DB but not in the registry.
out_of_scope: Do not change registry generation in this task.
acceptance_criteria: Given an extra DB table not in the registry, when drift-detect runs, then exit code is non-zero and stderr names the extra table.
Given a fully-registered DB, when drift-detect runs, then exit code is 0.
test_strategy: RED: write drift-detect-strict.test.ts asserting both branches before flag enable.
risk_red_lines: Do not enable strict mode without a complete registry.
Do not auto-fix (drop the extra table) — only report.
